intervention_reason: Unity runtime verification (EditMode/PlayMode execution and StandaloneOSX build) still requires Unity license secrets in GitHub Actions and a local Unity Editor install for end-to-end manual validation.
notes: Environment/tooling observed during verification: git 2.39.5 (Apple Git-154), git-lfs 3.7.1, gh 2.87.3 (authenticated as gdelcarmen with repo/workflow scopes), dotnet CLI unavailable locally, Unity CLI/editor unavailable locally. Remote sync: repository gdelcarmen/dont-let-them-in exists with branches main and codex/sweep-2026-03-04, tag/release v0.0.1. CI inspection: CSharp Lint passed; Unity Build Check and Unity Tests failed only due to missing UNITY_LICENSE/UNITY_SERIAL secrets. Decisions implemented: (1) keep GameCI unity-builder and unity-test-runner workflows, (2) add explicit license detection and conditional skip messaging when Unity secrets are absent, (3) pass through UNITY_LICENSE/UNITY_SERIAL/UNITY_EMAIL/UNITY_PASSWORD to support both file and serial activation modes, (4) upload Unity artifacts only when Unity jobs execute, (5) document CI gating behavior in README.
